title: Unidentified or Venomous Snake Bite
immediate_steps: Call emergency services immediately
Keep the victim completely still and calm to slow venom spread
Remove jewelry and tight clothing before swelling begins
Immobilize the bitten area with a splint
Keep the bitten area at or slightly below heart level
Carry the victim if possible instead of letting them walk
avoid: Do not cut or slash the bite
Do not suck the venom by mouth or with any device
Do not apply ice, cold packs, or water
Do not give the person alcohol, caffeine, or any pain medication
Do not try to catch the snake

title: Non-Venomous Snake Bite
immediate_steps: Wash the bite area with soap and water
Apply an antibiotic ointment if available
Cover with a clean, dry bandage
Monitor for signs of infection over the next few days
Seek medical attention to verify it was non-venomous and for a possible tetanus shot
avoid: Do not ignore signs of infection (pus, severe redness)

title: Spitting Cobra - Venom in Eyes
symptoms: Redness and swelling around the eyes
immediate_steps: Immediately flush the eyes with large amounts of clean water or saline for at least 15-20 minutes
Blink frequently while flushing
Call emergency services immediately
Keep the person calm
avoid: Do not use eye drops or any chemicals to rinse the eyes (use only clean water or saline)

title: Snake Bite - Immediate Response
immediate_steps: Remain calm and avoid panic
Keep the affected person still
Remove any tight items such as rings, bracelets, or tight clothing near the affected area
Keep the bitten limb at or slightly below heart level
Seek emergency medical help immediately
avoid: Do not attempt to suck out the venom or cut the wound
Do not allow movement as it can spread venom faster through the body

title: Snake Bite - Do's and Don'ts
immediate_steps: Do keep the person calm and immobilized to slow venom spread
Do keep the affected limb still and slightly lowered
Do seek immediate medical attention
avoid: Do not cut the wound or try to suck out venom
Do not apply ice, chemicals, or herbal remedies
Do not allow the victim to walk or run
Avoid giving alcohol or painkillers unless prescribed by a medical professional

title: Snake Bite - Identification and Symptoms
immediate_steps: Medical evaluation is always necessary even if the bite appears minor (some bites may not show immediate symptoms)

title: Snake Bite - Stabilization and Transport
immediate_steps: Keep the person lying down and prevent any unnecessary movement
Immobilize the affected limb using a splint or cloth to reduce motion
Monitor breathing and consciousness levels continuously
If possible, note the time of the bite and description of the snake for medical reference
Ensure emergency services are contacted immediately
avoid: Do not risk further exposure to identify the snake
